name: Pickled Mixed Vegetables

ingredients.item: medium head cauliflower (about 1⅓ pounds/600 g), cut into bite-size florets
medium carrots, peeled and cut into flower-shaped slices (see Note)
medium cucumbers, halved, seeds removed, and cut into ¼- to ½-inch (0.6 to 1 cm) thick slices
large cloves garlic, green cores removed and thinly sliced lengthwise
bird’s eye or cayenne chili peppers, sliced or whole

method: In a large pourable jug, combine all ingredients for the brine and stir until solids are dissolved.
In a large bowl, combine cauliflower, carrots, cucumber, and salt. Toss well and let sit until moisture is drawn out of vegetables (about 30 minutes).
Rinse with cold water two or three times to remove excess salt, and then drain thoroughly.

Arrange vegetables, garlic, and chili peppers in two 47-ounce (1.4 L) sterilized glass jars (here), and then cover with brine. Use two bamboo skewers to press the vegetables into the brine. Close the lid tightly and store in a cool, dry place. After 1 to 2 days, they will be ready to eat.

Store in the refrigerator for up to 1 month. (If the pickles start to turn too sour while storing them, simply drain all the liquid and store in an airtight container or a fresh glass jar in the refrigerator.)
